properties.remark: POWER LINES DOWN IN NOTUS, GREENLEAF AND CALDWELL.
A SPOTTER IN GLENNS FERRY REPORTED A DUST STORM WITH VISIBILITIES LESS THAN 1/8 SM ALONG HIGHWAY 30.

MOUNTAIN HOME ASOS...MUO...REPORTED BLOWING DUST AND HAZE WITH WIND GUSTS BETWEEN 46 TO 54 KTS BETWEEN 01 AND 02Z. CURRENT OBSERVATIONS INDICATE WIND GUSTS TO 38 KTS. VISIBILITIES 1 TO 1 1/4 SM 011Z TO 0135Z.

TWIN FALLS ASOS REPORTED HZ WITH VISIBILITIES BETWEEN 1 3/4SM AND 5 SM BETWEEN 0315 AND 0348Z. WIND GUSTS OF UP TO 39 KTS WERE ALSO REPORTED DURING THIS TIME PERIOD. CURRENT REPORT OF GUSTS TO 31 KTS.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTED ONE INCH HAIL AND WIND GUSTS TO 50 MPH.
SMALL BRANCHES 1/2 INCH TO 3/4 INCH DIAMETER DOWN.
OFF-DUTY NEW EMPLOYEE REPORTED WIND GUSTS TO 60 MPH
OFF-DUTY NWS EMPLOYEE REPORTED WIND GUSTS TO 60 MPH
TREES DOWN IN NW NAMPA NEAR MOSS LANE AND PORTNER ROAD. RADAR ESTIMATED WIND SPEED 45 MPH AND TIME 640 PM. KEUL WIND GUST 39 KTS AT 0035 UTC OR 635 PM MDT.
INTERSECTION OF 3RD AND IDAHO STREET 10 INCH DIAMETER BRANCH DOWN ABOUT 40 FEET LONG. NEWS STAND NEAR BY BLOWN OVER.
BOISE AIRPORT ASOS REPORTED AN ESTIMATED WIND GUST TO 58 MPH. WIND SENSOR APPEARED INOPERABLE AFTER A GUST TO 51 AT 0040Z...UNTIL THE PEAK WIND OF 58 MPH GUST WAS ESTIMATED AT 0049Z.
6800 CUSTOMERS WITH OUT POWER IN BOISE, EAGLE, MERIDIAN, KUNA AND STAR.
POWER OUTAGES TO 3100 CUSTOMERS IN BOISE...MERIDIAN...EAGLE AND STAR.

FIRST THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW REACHED KMUO AFB AT 113Z WITH WINDS 45G59MPH AND VISIBILITY OF 0.75 MILE. WINDS DECREASED SLIGHTLY TO 40G52MPH 131Z-136Z THEN PICKED UP AGAIN TO 75 AT 208-209Z. BY 220Z WINDS FROM THE SECOND OUTFLOW HAD DROPPED BACK TO 41G54 MPH.
MOUNTAIN HOME RAWS REPORTED WIND GUSTS TO 60 MPH.

THE FIRST OUTFLOW BROUGHT A GUST TO 60 MPH THEN A SECOND SURGE OF OUTFLOW WINDS BROUGHT WIND GUSTS TO 56MPH AT MESONET SITE KU76. 0115Z 39G60 MPH...0135Z 32G56 MPH...155Z 31G45 MPH THEN 0215Z 45G56 MPH. DIRECTION VARIED BETWEEN W AND NW.VISIBILILTY DROPPED BRIEFLY TO 2.5 MILES.
THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW WINDS BROUGHT 65 MPH TO LUCKY PEAK RAWS IN THE HOUR ENDING AT 131Z AND NEARBY THUNDERSTORMS BROUGHT A 67 MPH GUST IN THE HOUR ENDING AT 231Z.

MOUNTAIN HOME RAWS PEAK WIND OF 62 MPH OCCURRED IN THE HOUR ENDING AT 0152Z. A PEAK WIND OF 60 OCCURRED INTHE HOUR ENDING AT 252Z. NO PRECIP WAS RECORDED AND THE TEMPERATURE DROPPED FROM 96 TO 78 IN THE 2 HRS ENDING AT 252Z. GUST DIRECTION FROM THE NNW.
MOUNTAIN HOME AIRFORCE BASE HAD A PEAK GUST TO 75 MPH.
A SECOND ROUND OF THUNDERSTORM WIND GUSTS BROUGHT THE PEAK GUST OF THE EVENT TO LUCKY PEAK RAWS...67 MPH.

TWIN BUTTES RAWS WIND WNW 43 GUSTING TO 61 MPH AT 255Z WITH TEMPERATUR DROPPING 14F IN THE LAST HOUR. WINDS REMAINED UP AT 355Z FROM THE NW 27G51 MPH THEN ANOTHER SURGE OF THUNDERSTORM OUTFLOW WIND GUSTS BROUGHT ANOTHER 8F COOLING AND WSW WINDS OF 49 G 64 MPH AT 455Z.
ROOF LIFTED OFF OF BARN AND TOSSED SEVERAL HUNDRED YARDS AS IT SLOWLY DISINTEGRATED. SEVERAL ANIMALS HURT BY FLYING DEBRIS.
TREE BRANCHES 2-4 INCHES IN DIAMETER BROKEN OFF OF TREES.
WIND CAUSED POWER OUTAGE TO 6000 CUSTOMERS IN MERIDIAN...BOISE AND EAGLE.
TOWN CREEK RAWS REPORTED GUST TO 90 MPH
TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN ALONG HARRIS CREEK ROAD.
TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN IN WILDERNESS RANCH AREA
LARGE TREES BLOWN DOWN AND POWERLINES DOWN AT PINE FLAT CAMPGROUND WEST OF LOWMAN
TWO WASHOUTS ACROSS WARM LAKE HIGHWAY
SEVERAL MUDSLIDES CLOSE FOREST ROAD 48 FROM MCCALL TO YELLOW PINE. POWERLINES ARE DOWN AND SEVERAL MOTORIST WERE STRANED ON THE ROAD.
EYEWITNESS REPORT OF A TORNADO ON THE GROUND FOR 30 SECONDS 10 MILES SOUTH OF TWIN FALLS...OR 4 MILES SOUTH OF THE TWIN FALLS AIRPORT.
DAMAGE TO CARS AND PIVOT IRRIGATION SYSTEMS.
SEVERAL TREES AND POWER LINES DOWN.
